name: EarthWorx NW1000 Non-Woven Geotextile - 4.5m x 100m
description:
EarthWorx NW1000 Non-Woven Geotextile - 4.5m x 100m
 

An extra heavy duty, non-woven geotextile with superior durability and mechanical resistance, providing ultimate performance in separation, filtration, drainage and reinforcement making it widely used for roads, highways, car parks, railway construction, foundations and retaining structures.

EarthWorx NW1000 is manufactured from 100% polypropylene fibres to a high specification, which makes it suitable for demanding civil engineering projects particularly those with areas subject to a large volume of vehicular traffic and large imposed loads. This prevents the sub-grade (soil) and sub-base (MOT Type 1 or similar) from mixing, which leads to contamination of the stone sub-base resulting in sinking and rutting of the surface.

Appliations

	Car Parks

	Retaining Structures
	Horse Arenas
	Highways

name: 150mm Heritage Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Eared Slip Coupling
description:
150mm Heritage Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Eared Slip Coupling
 

Saint-Gobain PAM UK Cast Iron Timesaver Soil Pipe System is Available in a range of pipe diameters with an exstensive range of fittings and to suit any project.

Saint-Gobain PAM UK are market leaders in the supply of Cast iron soil, drainage and rainwater systems.

Benefits of Cast Iron


	Non-combustible fire rated A1
	Excellent acoustic properties
	Minimal expansion
	Strong and robust
	Excellent environmental properties
	Made from up to 99% recycled content
	100% recyclable
	Long life span


Timesaver Soil Coating


	3m Pipes Internally lined with an ochre two-part epoxy coating
	Externally coated in a black acrylic paint.
	Fittings and couplings, Internally and externally coated in a black waterbased paint
	The Timesaver coating shall accept over-coating (Overpainting) with alkyd and water based paints normally used on metallic structures

The Cast Iron Soil Pipe Timesaver range include heritage push-fit couplings that turns a mechanical pipe system into a system with the traditional cast iron appearance.

The Timesaver Heritage couplings have been accepted by recognised bodies for all grade listed properties ie. National Heritage, English Heritage etc. Its primer black coating makes it easy to overpaint for external soil stacks, and is the perfect solution for listed buildings and those situated in areas of conservation.

Prestigious commercial buildings built in the 1970’s to the mid 90’s which are serviced by cast iron soil stacks, will most likely be Timesaver. If the building is to be refurbished and changes are required, the latest Timesaver range is best suited to connect to the original pipework.

Please Note: Timesaver soil fittings were traditionally supplied with a raised bead on the spigot ends, which over the years have been removed. The Heritage couplings can only be used on fittings without the bead.

Manufacturer: Saint-Gobain PAM UK

Product Code: 266538

name: DrainTex Non-Woven Geotextile Fabric
description:
DrainTex Geotextile Membrane

Draintex is a standard duty, non-woven drainage geotextile fabric with excellent drainage and filtration properties and is avaiable in 1m x 10m, 2m x 50m, 2m x 25m, 4.5m x 11.1m and 4.5m x 100m.

This drainage geo-textile fabric has a stable but open structure making it ideal for lining trenches when constructing land (French) drains.

Draintex acts as a filter membrane, separating the aggregate from the soil, thus preventing the aggregate from mixing with the soil and reducing the efficiency and capacity of the drain.

DrainTex is also used for wrapping soakaway and water attenuation crates in both small domestic and large commercial projects. The high permeability of the fabric means water can percolate through whilst preventing the soakaway becoming ‘silted’ up.

DrainTex can be used as a ground stabilisation membrane beneath MOT Type 1 or 3 aggregate or hardcore to form a stable base in many hard landscaping and civil engineering applications.

Appliations


	French/Land Drains
	Wrapping Soakaway Crates
	Driveway Subase

name: DrainTex Non-Woven Geotextile Fabric
description:
DrainTex Geotextile Membrane

Draintex is a standard duty, non-woven drainage geotextile fabric with excellent drainage and filtration properties and is avaiable in 1m x 10m, 2m x 50m, 2m x 25m, 4.5m x 11.1m and 4.5m x 100m.

This drainage geo-textile fabric has a stable but open structure making it ideal for lining trenches when constructing land (French) drains.

Draintex acts as a filter membrane, separating the aggregate from the soil, thus preventing the aggregate from mixing with the soil and reducing the efficiency and capacity of the drain.

DrainTex is also used for wrapping soakaway and water attenuation crates in both small domestic and large commercial projects. The high permeability of the fabric means water can percolate through whilst preventing the soakaway becoming ‘silted’ up.

DrainTex can be used as a ground stabilisation membrane beneath MOT Type 1 or 3 aggregate or hardcore to form a stable base in many hard landscaping and civil engineering applications.

Appliations


	French/Land Drains
	Wrapping Soakaway Crates
	Driveway Subase

name: 150mm x 100mm Heritage Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Pipe Taper
description:
150mm x 100mm Heritage Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Pipe Taper
 

Saint-Gobain PAM UK Cast Iron Timesaver Soil Pipe System is Available in a range of pipe diameters with an exstensive range of fittings and to suit any project.

Saint-Gobain PAM UK are market leaders in the supply of Cast iron soil, drainage and rainwater systems.

Benefits of Cast Iron


	Non-combustible fire rated A1
	Excellent acoustic properties
	Minimal expansion
	Strong and robust
	Excellent environmental properties
	Made from up to 99% recycled content
	100% recyclable
	Long life span


Timesaver Soil Coating


	3m Pipes Internally lined with an ochre two-part epoxy coating
	Externally coated in a black acrylic paint.
	Fittings and couplings, Internally and externally coated in a black waterbased paint
	The Timesaver coating shall accept over-coating (Overpainting) with alkyd and water based paints normally used on metallic structures

The Cast Iron Soil Pipe Timesaver range include heritage push-fit couplings that turns a mechanical pipe system into a system with the traditional cast iron appearance.

The Timesaver Heritage couplings have been accepted by recognised bodies for all grade listed properties ie. National Heritage, English Heritage etc. Its primer black coating makes it easy to overpaint for external soil stacks, and is the perfect solution for listed buildings and those situated in areas of conservation.

Prestigious commercial buildings built in the 1970’s to the mid 90’s which are serviced by cast iron soil stacks, will most likely be Timesaver. If the building is to be refurbished and changes are required, the latest Timesaver range is best suited to connect to the original pipework.

Please Note: Timesaver soil fittings were traditionally supplied with a raised bead on the spigot ends, which over the years have been removed. The Heritage couplings can only be used on fittings without the bead.

Manufacturer: Saint-Gobain PAM UK

Product Code: 191713

150mm Heritage Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Single Swept Branch 87.5 Degrees
 

Saint-Gobain PAM UK Cast Iron Timesaver Soil Pipe System is Available in a range of pipe diameters with an exstensive range of fittings and to suit any project.

Saint-Gobain PAM UK are market leaders in the supply of Cast iron soil, drainage and rainwater systems.

Benefits of Cast Iron


	Non-combustible fire rated A1
	Excellent acoustic properties
	Minimal expansion
	Strong and robust
	Excellent environmental properties
	Made from up to 99% recycled content
	100% recyclable
	Long life span


Timesaver Soil Coating


	3m Pipes Internally lined with an ochre two-part epoxy coating
	Externally coated in a black acrylic paint.
	Fittings and couplings, Internally and externally coated in a black waterbased paint
	The Timesaver coating shall accept over-coating (Overpainting) with alkyd and water based paints normally used on metallic structures

The Cast Iron Soil Pipe Timesaver range include heritage push-fit couplings that turns a mechanical pipe system into a system with the traditional cast iron appearance.

The Timesaver Heritage couplings have been accepted by recognised bodies for all grade listed properties ie. National Heritage, English Heritage etc. Its primer black coating makes it easy to overpaint for external soil stacks, and is the perfect solution for listed buildings and those situated in areas of conservation.

Prestigious commercial buildings built in the 1970’s to the mid 90’s which are serviced by cast iron soil stacks, will most likely be Timesaver. If the building is to be refurbished and changes are required, the latest Timesaver range is best suited to connect to the original pipework.

Please Note: Timesaver soil fittings were traditionally supplied with a raised bead on the spigot ends, which over the years have been removed. The Heritage couplings can only be used on fittings without the bead.

Manufacturer: Saint-Gobain PAM UK

Product Code: 191664

name: 100mm Heritage Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Strap On Boss
description:
100mm Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Strap On Boss
 

Saint-Gobain PAM UK Cast Iron Timesaver Soil Pipe System is Available in a range of pipe diameters with an exstensive range of fittings and to suit any project.

The strap-on boss provides a simple solution for fitting a 50mm copper or waste pipe to an existing 100mm cast iron soil pipe to BS 416 (pipe outside diameter min/max 109/114mm).

Installation


	Simply determine where the waste pipe is to be positioned.
	Cut a 64mm hole into the cast iron soil pipe with a hole saw (the metal from the hole remains in the cutter – see tools below).
	Mechanically fit the boss strap in position (do not forget the rubber washer) tighten until fully secure.
	Insert in the waste pipe until fully seated in the boss.
	Tighten the boss plate to grip the rubber ‘O’ ring on the outside of the waste pipe.


Tools required


	A 64mm hole saw: Product code 192326.
	Arbour: Product code 192327.
	¼" pilot drill: Product code 192328.
	13mm socket EF101: Product code 191202

or


	13mm spanner for mechanically fitting the boss adaptor EF098: Product code 191200.

	Non-combustible fire rated A1
	Excellent acoustic properties
	Minimal expansion
	Strong and robust
	Excellent environmental properties
	Made from up to 99% recycled content

Timesaver Soil Coating


	3m Pipes Internally lined with an ochre two-part epoxy coating
	Externally coated in a black acrylic paint.
	Fittings and couplings, Internally and externally coated in a black waterbased paint
	The Timesaver coating shall accept over-coating (Overpainting) with alkyd and water based paints normally used on metallic structures

The Cast Iron Soil Pipe Timesaver range include heritage push-fit couplings that turns a mechanical pipe system into a system with the traditional cast iron appearance.

The Timesaver Heritage couplings have been accepted by recognised bodies for all grade listed properties ie. National Heritage, English Heritage etc. Its primer black coating makes it easy to overpaint for external soil stacks, and is the perfect solution for listed buildings and those situated in areas of conservation.

Prestigious commercial buildings built in the 1970’s to the mid 90’s which are serviced by cast iron soil stacks, will most likely be Timesaver. If the building is to be refurbished and changes are required, the latest Timesaver range is best suited to connect to the original pipework.

Please Note: Timesaver soil fittings were traditionally supplied with a raised bead on the spigot ends, which over the years have been removed. The Heritage couplings can only be used on fittings without the bead.
